title: GH #17: write-guard denies code files under docs/history/ + prose (verify scripts belong in project/spikes)
action: Agents were writing verify.sh scripts into docs/history/<feature>/ (image: vnbptw-mapcompany during validating). docs/history/ is the tech-agnostic knowledge layer (.md only). Guard: checkWrite now denies a code-extension file (.sh/.mjs/.py/.js/.ts/.rb/... via HISTORY_CODE_EXTENSIONS) under docs/history/, kind='docs-history-code', message pointing to the project's own scripts (persistent verify) or .bee/spikes/ (disposable). Prose: bee-validating Spike Rules gains a note that verify scripts + executable code never go in docs/history. Test-first: RED (a .sh under docs/history was allowed) -> GREEN.

truths: checkWrite denies a code-extension file under docs/history/ in every phase (kind docs-history-code).
.md/.json under docs/history/ stay allowed; code outside docs/history/ is unaffected by this rule.
bee-validating prose says verify scripts live in project scripts or .bee/spikes/, never docs/history.
artifacts: Full 21-suite verify green; RED->GREEN checkWrite test.
prohibitions: Do NOT change the intake/lane/hold logic; only add the code-in-history rule.

trace.outcome: write-guard denies code files under docs/history (verify scripts belong in project scripts or .bee/spikes/); bee-validating prose reinforces it
trace.verification_evidence:
Before (GH #17, image from vnbptw-mapcompany during bee-validating): an agent ran Write(docs/history/industry-count-company-registered/verify.sh) — a 58-line bash verification harness — into the docs/history/ knowledge layer. bee's design says disposable code -> .bee/spikes/, and docs/history/ is .md-only (CONTEXT/plan/reports/walkthrough), but nothing DENIED code there, so agents improvised. RED: checkWrite(root, validating-state, 'docs/history/f/verify.sh') returned {allow:true}.
After: checkWrite denies any HISTORY_CODE_EXTENSIONS file (.sh/.bash/.ps1/.mjs/.cjs/.js/.jsx/.ts/.tsx/.py/.rb/.go/.rs/.java/.php/.pl/.lua/.r) under docs/history/, kind 'docs-history-code', with a message pointing to the project's own scripts (persistent verify) or .bee/spikes/ (disposable). bee-validating Spike Rules gains the matching prose.
Evidence: test_lib 339/0 — RED first (.sh allowed), GREEN after (denied); .md and .json under docs/history stay allowed; a .sh OUTSIDE docs/history is unaffected. test_write_guard + test_lib_mirror green; full 21-suite foreground CHAIN_EXIT:0; mirrors byte-identical. Scope frozen: intake/lane/hold logic untouched, only the code-in-history rule added.
trace.verify_output: test_lib 339/0 incl the RED->GREEN checkWrite #17 test (.sh/.mjs/.py/.js under docs/history denied kind=docs-history-code; .md/.json allowed; .sh outside docs/history allowed); test_write_guard + test_lib_mirror green; full 21-suite foreground CHAIN_EXIT:0.
